channel.title: Movie on PostDoc Problems
channel.description: Recent content in Movie on PostDoc Problems

item.title: Mind Game
item.description:
Weird as fuck is the only was to describe this movie - at least at the start.

The movie follows a sad, little guy who is stuck in self-pity. Then some random events happen and he gets stuck with 3 other characters on a cast-away type of situation. The rest of the movie is the 4 of them shedding their fake shells and discovering their true selves. I have been thinking about these same things lately and this movie almost feels like it’s written personally for me.

item.title: Godzilla Minus One
item.description: I never imagined that I would like a Kaiju movie. I never understood about their point or their attraction. But Godzilla Minus One was a really good movie. The Godzilla is a metaphor for nuclear holocaust, a force of nature, a tragedy too big to control. Even though it is the central plo point and the story revolves around it, it simply presents the background in which the characters evolve. Godzilla minus one is really the story of a failed Kamikaze pilot who finally finds courage and closure and a way to reconcile with his past. And also a peek into the aftermath of WW2 in Tokyo.

item.title: Poor Things
item.description: After watching the trailer, I was very reluctant to watch Poor Things. But one boring Saturday I had nothing else to do so I watched it in a theater and am I glad I did. What a visual feast! There was not a dull moment in the movie. It felt like I would miss something important if I blinked. Emma Watson and Mark Ruffalo are an absolute delight to watch. Now I need to watch other movies by this genius director.

item.title: Anatomy of a Fall
item.description: Watch for the impressive acting and the gripping story.

item.title: Glass onion
item.description:
Sequel to Knives Out.

It must be very difficult to make a good detective movie today, since there has already been so many such movies and the entire point of a detective movie is in the suspense. If the audience gets even so much as a clue of how the mystery is resolved the entire movie gets spoiled. But glass onion manages to surmount this steep challenge with ease.

item.title: Decision to leave
item.description:
Made by the same director who directed the Vengeance trilogy and the Handmaiden.

The actress, Tang Wei, is phenomenal. Throughout the movie you cannot make up your mind as to whether she is the victim or a femme fatale or if those two are even meaningfully distinguishable. The ease and authenticity with which she (and the actor) acts in some of the intense scenes is quite disturbing.
